pairs.hazard: ClickHouse lower() is ASCII-only (İ/ß unchanged); Trino lower() does full Unicode lower with locale folding (İ->i+U+0307). Neither matches; use lowerUTF8 to approach Trino.
ClickHouse upper() ASCII-only; Trino upper() full folding (ß->SS). upperUTF8 gives ß->SS (matches Trino) but is still ASCII-passthrough for the plain upper name.
ClickHouse length()=BYTE count; Trino length() = code-point count. Use lengthUTF8/char_length for parity.
ClickHouse reverse()=byte reverse (mangles UTF-8); Trino reverse is code-point-aware. reverseUTF8 approaches Trino (code points) but not grapheme clusters.
ClickHouse trim strips only ASCII space; Trino trim (Java Character.isWhitespace) strips tab/LF/CR/FF/VT too. Different whitespace set.
Same whitespace-set divergence as trim.
Same whitespace-set divergence as trim.

ClickHouse concat PROPAGATES NULL -> NULL (matches Trino's NULL propagation). This pair is ALIGNED on NULL — unlike DuckDB. Verdict divergent only for the string-vs-array concat overload shape; NULL algebra matches Trino.
ClickHouse concat_ws propagates NULL element -> NULL; Trino concat_ws SKIPS NULL elements. Divergent on NULL elements; separator-NULL -> NULL in both.
ClickHouse substring is BYTE-indexed and start=0 -> ''; Trino substring is code-point-indexed, 1-based. substringUTF8 fixes multibyte only.
See substr: byte indexing + start=0 differ from Trino code-point semantics.
ClickHouse replace == replaceAll (all occurrences); Trino replace(s,from,to) also replaces all. Aligned.
ClickHouse regexp_replace = replaceRegexpAll (ALL matches); Trino regexp_replace also replaces ALL by default. Aligned (both differ from DuckDB's first-only). RE2 in both.
Group 0 = whole match in both; RE2 engine in both.
ClickHouse leftPad pads by BYTES; Trino lpad pads by code points. leftPadUTF8 for parity.
ClickHouse rightPad pads by BYTES; Trino rpad by code points. rightPadUTF8 for parity.
ClickHouse soundex('Robert')='R163'; Trino soundex same American Soundex. Aligned.
Standard alphabet in both.
Aligned decode.
Same digest; ClickHouse MD5 -> raw bytes (FixedString), Trino md5 -> VARBINARY. Both binary (unlike DuckDB hex) but distinct types; hex() to compare.
Same digest; ClickHouse raw bytes vs Trino VARBINARY.
Same digest; raw-bytes vs VARBINARY return.
ClickHouse SHA512 present; same digest as Trino sha512; raw-bytes vs VARBINARY.
ClickHouse CRC32('abc')=891568578; Trino crc32 returns the same CRC-32 (bigint). (DuckDB has no crc32.)
Both compute XXH64; ClickHouse returns UInt64, Trino xxhash64 returns VARBINARY(8) — same bits, different type/signedness. (DuckDB has no xxhash64.)
abs(INT_MIN): ClickHouse widens to a positive value; Trino throws on the overflow.
modulo by zero: ClickHouse throws; Trino also throws (division_by_zero). Non-zero aligned — this pair is actually aligned on error behavior; divergent flag reserved for the wrap/promotion axis.
ClickHouse round() = banker's (half-even); Trino round() = half-UP (round half away from zero for positives). round(2.5)=2 vs 3.
ClickHouse truncate/trunc(x,n) truncates toward zero; Trino truncate(x,n) same intent. Aligned on the probe (2.678,2 -> 2.67).
ClickHouse log(x)=natural log (== Trino ln). Value-correct rename, but domain: ln(0)/ln(-1) -> ClickHouse NULL vs Trino error/NaN.
ClickHouse single-arg log = natural log; Trino log(b,x) is base-b (two-arg). Signature + base collision.
Base-10 log aligned.
log2(8)=3 aligned.
pow(-8,0.5): ClickHouse NULL; Trino NaN. Finite real cases aligned.
See pow: complex result NULL vs NaN; finite aligned.
sqrt(-1): ClickHouse NULL; Trino NaN.
Aligned on integer/zero probes.
ClickHouse e()=2.718281828459045; Trino e() same constant.
Aligned constant.
Aligned to double precision.
cbrt(27)=3 aligned.
acos(2) out of domain: ClickHouse NULL; Trino throws/NaN. In-domain aligned.
asin(2) out of domain: ClickHouse NULL; Trino throws/NaN. In-domain aligned.
Aligned.
Aligned.
Aligned.
Aligned.
Aligned.
Aligned.
Aligned.
Aligned.
Aligned.
Aligned.
ClickHouse widthBucket present and matched the probe (5 over [0,10]/5 bins -> 3); Trino width_bucket same intent. camelCase name is case-sensitive. (DuckDB lacked width_bucket in this build.)
1970+ aligned; ClickHouse Date/DateTime floors at 1970 so pre-1970 timestamps that Trino handles will clamp.
Aligned for representable dates.
Aligned for representable dates.
Aligned with matched session zones; ClickHouse DateTime is timezone-typed vs Trino's timestamp-with/without-zone distinction.
Aligned.
Aligned.

DuckDB/Trino millisecond(t) = seconds-within-minute*1000 + ms; the generator now emits (toSecond(t)*1000 + toMillisecond(t)), result-identical (ClickHouse toMillisecond alone is the sub-second component only). Verified: 30123/0/5789/56001 all match.
pairs.provenance: composed: clickhouse-live + trino-duckdb-hazards | REPORT-clickhouse-differential-probe-2026-07-13.md#datetime-millisecond | generator mapping fixed 2026-07-13 (BUGS row 6), live-differential-verified vs ClickHouse 26.5.1.1 + DuckDB 1.5.4

pairs.hazard: Aligned.
Trino week (week_of_year) is ISO-8601; the generator now emits toISOWeek, result-identical (not toWeek mode 0).
pairs.provenance: composed: clickhouse-live + trino-duckdb-hazards | REPORT-clickhouse-differential-probe-2026-07-13.md#datetime-week-mode | generator mapping fixed 2026-07-13 (BUGS-clickhouse-generator-mappings); re-verify vs live FE

pairs.hazard: Aligned on the intersecting unit set with matched zones; ClickHouse unit is a string first-arg and DateTime is tz-typed; 1970 floor applies.
Both take (unit, a, b); aligned on day diff. ClickHouse tz-typed operands; 1970 floor.

SPEC DIALECT + name: Trino date_format uses Joda/MySQL patterns; ClickHouse formatDateTime uses MySQL-style %-codes where %M is the MONTH NAME (probe: '%M' -> 'March') — colliding with strftime %M=minute. Never map format strings by passthrough.
Both epoch-seconds -> timestamp; ClickHouse returns a timezone-typed DateTime (session zone) and Trino from_unixtime(double) returns timestamp(3) with zone. Zone/precision-sensitive.
ClickHouse toUnixTimestamp(dt) -> UInt32 seconds (session zone); Trino to_unixtime -> double seconds. Value aligned when zones match; type differs.
Cast-to-date aligned for 1970+ (2024-06-15); ClickHouse Date floors at 1970.
Trino cardinality(array) = element count; ClickHouse length(array) same. (ClickHouse also has cardinality as an alias.) Aligned for arrays; Trino cardinality(map) also -> ClickHouse length(map).
No direct ClickHouse array_remove; idiom is arrayFilter(x->x!=v, arr). NULL-element handling and the lambda shape differ from Trino's element-equality remove.
One-level flatten aligned.

NAME COLLISION: Trino transform(array, lambda) maps a lambda over elements; ClickHouse transform(x, from_array, to_array, default) is a scalar VALUE-REMAP (CASE-like) — completely different function. ClickHouse's lambda map is arrayMap.
Trino ngrams(array, n) returns an array of n-element sub-arrays; ClickHouse ngrams(string,n) returns overlapping byte/char substrings of a STRING. Different input/output shape.
Trino map(array_k, array_v) matches ClickHouse map(k1,v1,...) in intent but the constructor SHAPE differs (two arrays vs flat kv list).
Empty group -> 0 (ClickHouse) vs Trino NULL; ClickHouse sum overflow-wraps fixed-width ints where Trino throws/decimals. All-NULL -> NULL in both.
Empty group -> nan (ClickHouse) vs Trino NULL.
count(expr) skips NULL; count() empty -> 0 in both.
Empty group -> type default (ClickHouse) vs Trino NULL.
Empty group -> type default (ClickHouse) vs Trino NULL.
Bare stddev = sample in both.
Population stddev aligned.
Sample stddev aligned.
Population variance aligned.
Sample variance aligned.
Pearson correlation aligned.
Population covariance aligned.
Sample covariance aligned.
Trino histogram = exact value->count map; ClickHouse histogram(bins)(x) = approximate adaptive bins. Different shape.
Trino array_agg -> ClickHouse groupArray; ordering undefined without ORDER BY; groupArray drops NULLs.
Order-dependent/nondeterministic without ORDER BY in both.
Both approximate top-k; ClickHouse topK(k)(x) parametric signature and sketch differ from Trino approx_top_k — only the top-value set is comparable, not internal state.
Aligned.
Aligned.
Aligned.
Aligned.
Aligned on default frame.
Aligned on default running frame.
0,0.5,1 aligned.
Aligned.
ClickHouse lag fills the type default (0) out of partition; Trino lag returns NULL (default) unless a default is supplied.
ClickHouse lead fills type default (0) past the end; Trino returns NULL.
ClickHouse returns type default before the Nth row; Trino returns NULL.
Non-deterministic; ClickHouse rand() -> UInt32, Trino rand() -> double [0,1). Never pushable.
Query-stable; ClickHouse now() DateTime (sec, tz-typed) vs Trino now() timestamp(3) with zone.
Session/zone-dependent; ClickHouse Date (1970 floor).
ClickHouse timezone() -> session zone name; not a Trino-comparable scalar. Session-only.

Bare variance() DEFAULT differs: Doris is POPULATION; DuckDB/Trino SAMPLE (1.84 vs 2.3). ClickHouse has NO bare variance() (only varSamp/varPop) so it must be translated. Use var_pop/var_samp explicitly.

pairs.hazard: String aggregation agrees ONLY with an explicit ORDER BY (a,a,b,c, NULLs skipped); default ordering is unspecified and separator/function name differ per engine (string_agg/group_concat/listagg/arrayStringConcat).
